insight: ChatControl's Discord integration requires the DiscordSRV plugin. Users must disable DiscordSRV's built-in chat relay by setting `DiscordChatChannelMinecraftToDiscord: false` in DiscordSRV's `config.yml`, otherwise messages get duplicated. ChatControl checks this on startup and logs a warning if it's still enabled (see ChatControl.java line ~247).

In PlayerMessage.java's filter() method, a fallback block after the player loop resends the message to `wrappedSender` when no player received it. For timed messages, `wrappedSender` is set as a side effect of `TimedMessagesCheck.setVariablesFor()` during iteration, so this fallback bypasses the toggle/ignore check and sends to the last iterated player. This was fixed by excluding `PlayerMessageType.TIMED` from the fallback condition.
